first_call: ^Agent 0x99: If you need guidance on any challenges, I'm here. That's what handlers are for.
support_hub: ^Operation Shatter resolved — I'm ready for debrief
^I discovered what ENTROPY is planning - Operation Shatter
^Those files on Kevin's PC — what should I do with this?
^Derek's office is locked — how do I get in?
lockpick_help: ^Agent 0x99: Lockpicking is about patience and listening.
^Agent 0x99: Each pin has a sweet spot. Apply tension, test each pin, feel for the feedback.
^Agent 0x99: Start with the storage closet practice safe—low stakes, good for learning.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Don't force it. If you're stuck, reset and try again. There's no timer.

ssh_help: ^Agent 0x99: SSH brute force uses Hydra to test password lists against login prompts.
^Agent 0x99: The key is using good password lists. Kevin's hints about "ViralDynamics2025" variations are gold.
^Agent 0x99: Command format: hydra -l username -P passwordlist.txt ssh:
^What if I don't have a password list?

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Build one from intel. Kevin mentioned patterns, the whiteboard had clues. Social engineering works.

linux_help: ^Agent 0x99: Linux navigation basics: ls lists files, cd changes directory, cat reads files.
^Agent 0x99: Check the home directory first. User files, hidden configs—look for .bashrc, .ssh, personal directories.
^Agent 0x99: Hidden files start with a dot. Use ls -la to see them.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Home directories, user documents, sometimes hidden in config files. Explore methodically.

sudo_help: ^Agent 0x99: Privilege escalation means gaining access to other accounts or higher permissions.
^Agent 0x99: Try "sudo -l" to see what sudo permissions you have. Some accounts allow switching users.
^Agent 0x99: Command: sudo -u otherusername bash gives you a shell as that user.
^What if I don't have sudo access?

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Check for misconfigured files, world-writable directories, or SUID binaries. But for this mission, sudo works.

general_advice: ^Agent 0x99: Remember the mission priorities: gather evidence, identify operatives, minimize innocent casualties.
^Agent 0x99: Most people at Viral Dynamics are legitimate employees. We want ENTROPY, not collateral damage.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Evidence correlation. Look for encrypted communications, connections to known cells, suspicious behavior.
^Agent 0x99: Derek's our primary suspect, but gather proof before confronting.
c-1: ^Agent 0x99: Protect her. She's the informant who brought this to us. Don't expose her unless absolutely necessary.

shatter_details_2: ^Agent 0x99: Talk to me. What did Maya tell you?
^Over two million profiles. Fake hospital closures, bank failures, infrastructure attacks.
shatter_casualties: ^, this is worse than we thought.
^Their own projections: 42 to 85 deaths in the first 24 hours
^They've calculated acceptable casualties. They're targeting diabetics, elderly, people with anxiety disorders.
shatter_reaction: ^Agent 0x99: They're not just terrorists. They're mass murderers with spreadsheets.
^, listen carefully. Your mission just changed priority.
updated_objectives: ^Agent 0x99: New priority objective: Stop Operation Shatter before deployment.
^Agent 0x99: Find the complete documentation—target lists, message templates, deployment systems.
^Agent 0x99: Gather proof of Derek's involvement. And shut down their attack infrastructure before those messages go out.
people_at_stake: ^. Even if they don't know it.
^Agent 0x99: Diabetics who'll skip insulin. Elderly with heart conditions. People who'll panic and make fatal decisions.
^Agent 0x99: Every piece of evidence you find brings us closer to stopping this.
mission_commitment: ^Agent 0x99: Good work discovering this. Now we know what we're dealing with.
^Agent 0x99: Continue investigating. Find the Operation Shatter files, identify all operatives, and prepare to shut this down.
^Agent 0x99: Call me if you need support. This just became a race against the clock.
event_derek_office_locked: ^Agent 0x99: That's Derek's office — locked tight. You'll need a way in.
^Agent 0x99: A lockpick would do the trick. Kevin in IT might be able to help with that.
^Agent 0x99: Or there might be a spare key somewhere. Poke around the other offices.
^Got it, I'll find a way in

c-1: ^Agent 0x99: IT room, east side of the main office. You'll need the PIN to get in — check around for maintenance notes.

b: ^Agent 0x99: Got the lockpick kit. Direct approach — Kevin wasn't going to hand them over like that.
^Agent 0x99: I see Kevin gave you lockpicks. Smart social engineering.

event_lockpick_acquired: ^Agent 0x99: Practice on low-risk targets first. Storage closet, unlocked areas.
event_server_room_entered: ^Agent 0x99: You're in the server room. Good work getting access.
^Agent 0x99: Look for the compromised systems. VM access will give you deeper intelligence.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Evidence of ENTROPY's infrastructure. Backdoors, encrypted communications, anything linking Derek to other cells.

event_first_flag: ^Agent 0x99: Each flag unlocks intelligence. Keep correlating VM findings with physical evidence.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Continue the VM challenges, but don't forget physical investigation. Derek's office, filing cabinets, computer access.
^Agent 0x99: Hybrid approach—digital and physical evidence together.

event_derek_office_entered: ^Agent 0x99: Look for communications, project documents, anything linking him to ENTROPY.
^Agent 0x99: Whiteboard messages, computer files, filing cabinets. Be thorough.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Your cover is solid. You're doing a security audit—accessing offices is expected.
^Agent 0x99: But don't tip your hand too early. Gather evidence before confronting.

event_all_flags_submitted: ^Agent 0x99: All VM flags submitted. Excellent work.
^Agent 0x99: Intelligence confirms Derek Lawson as primary operative, coordinating with Zero Day Syndicate.
^Agent 0x99: Now correlate with physical evidence. Then we can move to confrontation.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: That's your call. Direct, silent extraction, or something creative.
^Agent 0x99: I trust your judgment. You've proven capable.

framing_evidence_briefing: ^. Not just the content — the details around it.
^Agent 0x99: Who filed what. Who signed off. Whether the headers actually match what they claim to be.
^Agent 0x99: Forensic markers are easy to overlook. They're also hard to fake perfectly.

c-1: ^Agent 0x99: Inconsistencies. Someone in the wrong role doing something outside their remit. A timestamp that doesn't add up. Authentication data the system itself has flagged.
^Agent 0x99: The files will tell you what they are — if you read them right.

event_contingency_found: ^, I just saw what you pulled from Derek's computer.
^Agent 0x99: He's planning to frame Kevin Park for the entire breach. Fake logs, forged emails, the works.
^Agent 0x99: Kevin—the IT guy who gave you access, who trusted you—is going to take the fall for ENTROPY.
contingency_reaction: ^Agent 0x99: It gets worse. Derek's contingency activates automatically when systems are seized.
^Agent 0x99: If we don't do something, Kevin gets arrested. His kids watch him taken away in handcuffs.
^Agent 0x99: Eventually he'd be cleared, but... that's not something you just walk off.
contingency_options: ^Agent 0x99: You have options here. None of them are perfect.
^Agent 0x99: What do you want to do?
^Warn Kevin directly - tell him what's coming
^Focus on the mission - Kevin's not my responsibility
confront_kevin_choice: ^Agent 0x99: So — present Derek's manufactured evidence to Kevin and see how he responds.
^Agent 0x99: If he's innocent, he'll know exactly what he's looking at. The anomaly report, the forged email — a good IT manager will spot the inconsistencies immediately.
^Agent 0x99: Just remember: we already know it's a setup. Whatever Kevin says, you decide what to believe.
^Agent 0x99: And if you decide to act on the false evidence anyway — that authority is yours. I won't stop you.
^Let's see what he says for himself

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Find Kevin. Show him what Derek planted. Then make the call.

warn_kevin_choice: ^Agent 0x99: Direct warning. Risky—if Kevin panics or acts differently, Derek might notice.
^Agent 0x99: But if it works, Kevin has time to lawyer up, document everything. He's protected.
^I'll take that risk. He deserves to know.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Understood. Find Kevin, tell him what's coming. Just... be careful how much you reveal.
^Agent 0x99: The more he knows about SAFETYNET, the more complicated this gets.

plant_evidence_choice: ^Agent 0x99: Anonymous help. Leave the frame-up files where our follow-up team will find them.
^Agent 0x99: Kevin never knows he was in danger. Investigators see Derek's setup immediately.
^Agent 0x99: Clean. Professional. Takes time, but lower risk.
^That's the smarter play. Do it that way.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Copy the contingency files to a visible location. Investigators will find them during evidence collection.
^Agent 0x99: Kevin walks away clean without ever knowing. That's the professional approach.

ignore_kevin_choice: ^Agent 0x99: Kevin helped you. If you ignore this, he gets arrested. His family watches.
^Agent 0x99: He'll be cleared eventually, but that's trauma that doesn't heal.
^The mission has to come first. I can't save everyone.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Just know that choice has consequences. For Kevin. For his family.
^Agent 0x99: And for you, when you think about it later.

event_act2_complete: ^Agent 0x99: You've identified the operatives and gathered the evidence.
^Agent 0x99: Time to decide: How do you want to resolve this?
^Agent 0x99: Confrontation, silent extraction, or public exposure. Each has consequences.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Take your time. This is the part where your choices matter most.

event_sarah_attacked: ^Agent 0x99: Sarah's a civilian—nothing operational about her. Her items will be on the floor when she goes down. The visitor badge and the main office key.
^Agent 0x99: You've got the authority. Keep moving.
^Needed the key. No time to explain.
event_sarah_ko: ^Agent 0x99: Check-in resolved. Sarah's key and badge are on the floor—pick them up and proceed.
^Agent 0x99: For the record: Sarah Martinez has no connection to ENTROPY. She's the receptionist.
^Agent 0x99: Collateral noted. Keep the mission moving.

c-1: ^Agent 0x99: I know. Field decisions rarely are. Go.

event_maya_attacked: ^Agent 0x99: Maya Chen is the one who contacted SAFETYNET. She's the reason we even know about Operation Shatter.
^Agent 0x99: You have the authority to make field calls. Just be aware of what you're losing.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: Noted. Your call. Whatever she knew about Shatter's inner workings goes with her if she goes down.
c-1: ^Agent 0x99: Then do it and keep moving. But understand what that costs us.

event_maya_ko: ^Agent 0x99: We may never know what Maya had to tell us.
^Agent 0x99: Whatever she'd gathered on Operation Shatter's inner workings—the names, the connections, the parts we don't have yet—that intelligence is gone.
^. Not ENTROPY. She came to us because she trusted SAFETYNET.
^Agent 0x99: You had the authority. The mission can still succeed. But we lost something today.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: It does. And it succeeded. Just... carry that one.
c-1: ^Agent 0x99: Honest answer. Focus on what's ahead—stop Operation Shatter. That's what Maya wanted.

event_kevin_attacked: ^Agent 0x99: That's one way to get the lockpick,
^Agent 0x99: You've got full operational authority—do whatever it takes to complete the mission. Just try to minimise collateral where you can.
^Agent 0x99: Kevin's items should drop when he goes down. Keep moving.
^He's not ENTROPY. Just in the way.

c-1: ^Agent 0x99: Correct. Kevin's clean. Innocent bystander in the wrong place. Happens in the field.
^Agent 0x99: Get what you need and keep pushing.

event_kevin_ko: ^Agent 0x99: Kevin's down. His items are on the floor—pick them up and continue.
^Agent 0x99: For the record: nothing in Kevin's files connects him to ENTROPY. He was just the IT guy trying to do his job.
^Agent 0x99: You had the authority to make that call. The debrief will note it—but this isn't a reprimand.

c-1: ^Agent 0x99: No. But field decisions rarely are. You've got the lockpick and keycard now—use them.

event_main_office_entered: ^Agent 0x99: You're in. Get a feel for the place.
^Agent 0x99: IT room is east — that's Kevin's territory. Break room is west. Start broad before you go deep.
^Agent 0x99: Desks, filing cabinets, notice boards. People leave more behind than they realise.

c-1: ^Agent 0x99: Kevin in the IT room can set you up with tools. But don't rush — context comes from exploration.

event_kevin_accused: ^Agent 0x99: Those logs pointing at Kevin were filed by Derek — a Marketing Manager submitting IT security reports and bypassing the IT Manager. That's not normal.
^Agent 0x99: Check Derek's office before you do anything you can't take back. I think you'll find the full picture there.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: I think Derek's been planning for someone to take the fall. Kevin's the obvious choice.
^Agent 0x99: But don't take my word for it. Find Derek's files. Then decide.

event_entropy_reveal_read: ^Agent 0x99: The Architect is real. ENTROPY is a network — nodes that don't know each other, each running independent operations.
^Agent 0x99: Derek is one node. Stopping Operation Shatter buys time. But The Architect is still out there.
^Agent 0x99: Whatever you choose to do with Derek — choose carefully. The way this ends sets a precedent.

c-0: ^Agent 0x99: We don't know. Not yet. That's the next mission.
^Agent 0x99: For now — you have Derek. Make it count.
c-1: ^Agent 0x99: You've done the hard work. Go end it.

closing_debrief: ^Agent 0x99: Operation Shatter is neutralized. Let's review what happened.
